intro: Holandrea nebrodensis, also known as Sardinian sage, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its grayish-green leaves and blue-purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Holandrea nebrodensis include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, often employed for their calming, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects.
